hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — K, 8. You raised aggressively before the flop, putting pressure on your opponent, who calmly called to see the next cards.
Flop — 10, 3, 4. You bet, trying to build the pot, and your opponent called, staying patient and uncommitted.
Turn — 7. You bet again, but this time your opponent raised, suddenly turning aggressive after hitting their card.
River — Ace. You checked, giving your opponent the opportunity to act, and they bet again, applying pressure with their made hand.

wrongReasons: J 7 has similar strength, but look at when the aggression shifted — the turn card connects with the correct hand in a way it doesn't connect with J 7.
5 2 makes a straight here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
9 Q doesn't benefit from the turn card the way the correct hand does. A player holding only high card wouldn't have the confidence to raise on that street.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 7 K

Your opponent **called** on the flop with no made hand, then the **7** on the turn gave them a pair, prompting an immediate **raise**. This change in behavior after the turn card is the key to their hand, as they went from passive to aggressive, indicating they had made **One Pair** with the **7**.
